NOTICE of SALE of REAL ESTATE Guardian

State of Oklahoma,
Tulsa County.
SS.
IN THE COUNTY COURT
In the matter of the Guardianship of
6 Daisy Floyd, John Hume and
Pete Williams, Jr., Minors.
Notice is bertby given in pursuance of an order of the County Court of the County of Tulsa, State of Oklahoma, made on the 4th day of November, 1920, the undersigned Guardian of the estate of said minors will sell at public sale to the highest bidder subject to confirmation by said Court, on Friday, the 26th day of November, A. D. 1920, at 9 o'clock a.m., at the front door of the County Curt House at Coweta, Wagoner County, Oklahoma, all the right title and interest of said minors in and to the following described real estate situated in Wagoner County, State of Oklahoma to-wit:
Lot fifteen (15), Block fifty-eight (58), New Coweta.
Said real estate will be sold on the following terms and conditions, to-wit: Cash in hand.
Bids for the purchase thereof must be in writing and must be filed in the County Court, or delivered to the undersigned at 114°1' N. Greenwood Street, Tulsa, Oklahoma.
Dated the 5th day of November 1920.

LULA WILLIAMS, Guardian
I. H SPEARS, Attorney for Estate

regions.label: paragraph_title
regions.text: MADERO SLAYERS ARRESTED

regions.label: text
regions.text:
Mexicans Planning to Prosecute Men For Murder of President.

Mexico City, Nov 12—General Ra Mtxico City, Nov. 12—General RafaelPimiento, who commanded the rurale guard in 1913 when president Madero and Vice President Jose Mario PinoSuarez were killed has been arrested. A government announcement says arrests of other persons of the assassination of Madero and Suarez are to follow.

One of the first acts of the present government was to order a complete investigation into the Madero case.

regions.label: paragraph_title
regions.text: FRENCH-CZECH TREATY SIGN'D

regions.label: text
regions.text: Czecho-Slovakia have concluded a commercial accord under which they mutually agree to re-establish freedom of trade between the two countries, so far as the exchange situation and revictualing necessities permit.

regions.label: paragraph_title
regions.text: JAPAN WITHDRAW TROOPS

regions.label: text
regions.text:
Associated Press
Tokio, Nov. 10—The cabinet, it is reported by the press today, has decided to withdraw the Japanese troops from the Chenteo area, along the Korean-Manchurian border, despite the fact that the activities of the bandits and Korean insurgents have not been suppressed. Tre with drawal would be contingent upon an understanding by China to maintain order and protect Japanese life and property. The diplomatic council has been summoned to consider the cabinet's proposal.

regions.label: paragraph_title
regions.text:
COUNTY AGENT'S PROMPT
WORK PREVENTS MOTOR OIL FAMINE

regions.label: text
regions.text:
Alertness on the part of the county agent in Umatilla County, Oreg., this year prevented a motor oil famine, which threatened many thousands of dollars in loss of crops.

Late in the spring it was found that due to an embargo there was little motor fuel on hand for crattors and trucks and that plowing could not be completed. Farmers used a distillate, a lower grade of gasoline, for tractors anl for operating the combined headers and thrashers with which the grain crops are harvested. Distillate was found to be entirely out of the market. The county agent appealed to several California refining Companies, and, with the aid of California State Marketing Bureau, succeeded in having about 25 carloads of distillate shipped into the region.

The orders of farmers were pooled and banks were instructed to honor demands on account of those in the pool. The first shipment of 12 carloads, or 1000,661 gallons, were distributed among 111 farmers in May and saved them $9,060. During June two more cars and July eight more cars were distributed making a total of 285,851 gallons of fuel. It is estimated that the entire savings totaled at least $15,000. During the month of June similar service was given farmers by county agents in Morrow, Sherman and Wasco Counties. In these three counties a total of 195,000 gallons of distillate were secured and distributed at a great saving.

Mrs. Corinne Graham formerly of the Muskogee City Schools, who has been supplementing her already expert knowledge in the modiste line of dressmaking by taking an extensive course in her art in the leading model shops of Chicago Ill., arrived in Tulsa last Wednesday, and is looking up a location for herself. She is the guest of Prof. and Mrs. Hughes of Detroit St.

regions.text: The greatest values ever offered in Trunks, Bags, and Leather Goods You will not be able to buy the same goods anywhere else in Tulsa at twice our prices. We invite inspection of our merchandise and comparison of prices.

regions.label: text
regions.text:
On account of sickness we are forced to quit business. We are actually marking our goods below cost in order to make quick disposition of our entire stock before cold weather comes. Here is your opportunity to buy high grade Leather Goods and Novelties for Christmas Gifts at substantial savings.

The people of Tulsa know the class of goods we have always handled, and should be eager o buy such fine merchandise at the extremely low prices which we quote on this page.

Such an enormous stock as ours could not be listed in one advertisement. We can only quote here a few articles in each line. If you don't see what you want on this page come in and let us show you a complete assortment. Anything you would expect to find in any Trunk or Leather Goods Store will be seen here. Remember, everything must go regardless of the loss to us. Yours is the gain. Buy quickly.
